leagueBriefing: It was a wild Monday on the diamond as mid-major programs and unranked challengers gave several powerhouses all they could handle. Stetson pulled off the day's biggest stunner by clinching a series split in Fayetteville, while Utah finished off a dominant sweep of Grand Canyon to keep their home momentum rolling. Meanwhile, high-scoring affairs in California and Florida proved that no lead is safe in midweek action.

games.headline: Stetson stuns No. 5 Arkansas to force rare series split at Baum-Walker
games.brief: Stetson jumped on Arkansas early and never looked back, silencing a Fayetteville crowd that isn't used to seeing the Razorbacks drop non-conference series at home. The Hatters tagged the Hogs' starter for four runs before he could record a single out, and while Arkansas clawed back within striking distance, their bats went cold when it mattered most. It was a gritty performance from the visitors that snapped a long-standing streak of home dominance for the ranked Razorbacks.
bullets: Freshman Carter Rutenbar was a lone bright spot for Arkansas, going 4-for-5 at the plate.
Stetson’s Yohann Dessureault set the tone immediately with a massive three-run homer in the first inning.
The Razorbacks struggled mightily with runners in scoring position, going 0-for-9 in those critical situations.
This marks the first time since 2014 that Arkansas has failed to win a non-conference weekend series at Baum-Walker Stadium.
games.context: The loss prevents Arkansas from building momentum heading into a high-stakes SEC opening weekend against Mississippi State.

games.headline: Utes complete clean sweep of Grand Canyon with steady series finale
games.brief: Utah didn't let a brief second-inning deficit dampen their spirits as they cruised to a 7-2 win, finishing a four-game sweep of Grand Canyon. The Utes' pitching has been nothing short of historic lately, and their bats backed them up on Monday with a balanced attack that saw every top-of-the-order hitter reach base. After a weekend of shutouts, the Utes proved they could handle a punch and still maintain control of the game.
bullets: Cameron Gurney provided the muscle for Utah with a 407-foot home run and four RBIs.
Utah's pitching staff has allowed only two runs over their last 41 innings of play.
The Utes' bullpen was perfect over the final four frames, allowing zero base runners to reach.
Dominic Chacon provided the only offense for GCU with a two-run homer in the second inning.
games.context: Utah enters the heart of their schedule riding a wave of pitching dominance that has them looking like a major threat in the Pac-12.

games.headline: CSUN bats explode in seven-run fifth to bury Pepperdine
games.brief: What started as a tight defensive battle quickly turned into a rout as Cal State Northridge dismantled Pepperdine in seven innings. The Matadors blew the game wide open in the fifth, putting up seven runs to trigger the mercy rule and send the Waves home early. It was a dominant display of situational hitting and aggressive baserunning that left Pepperdine with no answers on the road.
bullets: The game was called after seven innings due to a 10-run mercy rule after CSUN took an 11-run lead.
CSUN's Nathan Thomas delivered the knockout blow with a two-RBI triple during the decisive third inning.
Adam Christopher earned the win for the Matadors, keeping the Pepperdine bats in check throughout his outing.
Pepperdine managed only six hits and a single run, which came on a bases-loaded walk in the third.
games.context: The win pushes CSUN to 10-6 on the season as they continue to build a strong resume in the Big West.

games.headline: Oregon State flips the switch late to outlast Xavier in series finale
games.brief: Oregon State had to work much harder for this win than they did in Sunday's blowout, but a late surge helped the No. 18 Beavers pull away from Xavier to wrap up the series. While the Musketeers kept it close through the early innings, Oregon State's pitching depth and veteran composure proved too much to handle in the end. It wasn't the offensive firework show fans saw yesterday, but it was the kind of 'gritty victory' that ranked teams need to stack wins.
bullets: Starter Eric Segura was dominant, striking out eight over 5.2 scoreless innings.
The Beavers' pitching staff combined for 15 strikeouts on the night, consistently silencing Xavier's threats.
This victory concludes a four-game series that saw Oregon State showcase both power and defensive precision.
Oregon State has now won six consecutive games, continuing their climb up the national rankings.
games.context: The Beavers move to 11-4 and maintain their status as a top-tier contender as they prepare for conference play.

games.headline: Bulls survive 'Monday Madness' shootout to down Jacksonville
games.brief: South Florida and Jacksonville traded haymakers in a wild early-inning shootout that saw the game tied at five by the second inning. However, the Bulls' relentless offense eventually wore down the Dolphins, as USF scored in six different innings to pull away. It was a high-octane display of power hitting that saw both teams clear the fences early before USF's bullpen settled things down to secure the road win.
bullets: Matt Rose fueled the Bulls' offense with a massive three-run home run in the first inning.
Jacksonville’s Roger Vergara answered immediately with a two-run blast of his own in the bottom half.
USF's Jake Kulikowski had a stellar night, including a leadoff triple in the third that sparked a three-run rally.
The Bulls improved to 14-3 on the season, continuing one of the best starts in program history.
games.context: South Florida continues to look like a dangerous midweek opponent as they push toward a potential top-25 ranking.
